key_theorem.statement: The Cole-Hopf transform u = -2ν·∂_x(ln ψ) is an EML-2 map that reduces the nonlinear Burgers equation (EML-∞ solutions in the inviscid limit) to the linear heat equation (EML-3 solutions via the heat kernel). This is the EML analog of a 'linearization' or 'depth reduction' transformation.
key_theorem.corollary_kolmogorov: All Kolmogorov turbulence scales (η, v_η, τ_η, E(k)) are EML-2, as they are rational powers of the physical parameters (ν, ε).

ns_blowup.statement: NS solution blows up at T* iff vorticity ω(·,t) transitions from EML-finite (t < T*) to EML-∞ (t = T*).

bkm_criterion.statement: NS blowup at T* iff ∫₀^{T*} ‖ω(·,t)‖_{L∞} dt = ∞
bkm_criterion.eml_version: NS blowup at T* iff ‖ω(·,t)‖_{L∞} as a function of t transitions from EML-finite (t < T*) to EML-∞ (t → T*).
bkm_criterion.connection_to_phase_transition: Same structure as EML Phase Transition Theorem (Session 57): smooth phase ↔ EML-finite vorticity; singular phase ↔ EML-∞ vorticity.

vorticity_models.reason: Bounded by polynomial in t → EML-2
1/(T*-t) → ∞ at T*: diverges → EML-∞ at blowup
ln(1/(T*-t)) → ∞ but BKM criterion: ∫ln(1/(T*-t))dt < ∞ → NO blowup

ns_blowup.millennium_restatement: The NS Millennium Problem in EML language: Does there exist smooth initial data such that the NS vorticity transitions to EML-∞ at finite time T*? (Yes → blowup exists; No → global smooth solutions for all smooth initial data)

burgers_equation.heat_equation_for_psi: ψ_t = ν·ψ_xx [ψ is EML-3 via heat kernel]
burgers_equation.shock_eml: EML-∞ at shock: u becomes multivalued → infinite gradient

kolmogorov_scales.reason: All Kolmogorov scales = rational powers of (ν,ε) = exp(rational·ln(·)) = EML-2

taylor_green_vortex.vortex_stretching: ∂_t Ω = -Ω/Re + ∫u·S·ω dV [stretching term can overcome dissipation]

eml_depth_evolution.initial_condition_reason: sin(x)cos(y)cos(z) = product of EML-3 functions = EML-3

evolution.comment: EML-3 for small t; effective EML-∞ at large t when turbulence develops
EML-3 for small t; effective EML-∞ at large t when turbulence develops
EML-3 for small t; effective EML-∞ at large t when turbulence develops
EML-3 for small t; effective EML-∞ at large t when turbulence develops

eml_depth_evolution.transition_to_EML_inf: Occurs when vortex stretching exceeds viscous damping: Re >> 1

cole_hopf_eml_analysis.input: ψ solves heat equation ψ_t = ν·ψ_xx → EML-3 (heat kernel = erf)
cole_hopf_eml_analysis.output: u = Burgers solution → EML-3 (composition EML-2 ∘ EML-3)
cole_hopf_eml_analysis.depth_reduction: Cole-Hopf reduces the NONLINEAR Burgers (EML-∞ in inviscid limit) to the LINEAR heat equation (EML-3 solutions). The EML-2 map ln(ψ) acts as a 'depth linearizer'.
cole_hopf_eml_analysis.why_eml_2: ln is the EML-2 gate; ∂_x is depth-preserving → total EML-2 for the transform

cole_hopf_transform.description: u = -2ν·∂_x(ln ψ): transforms Burgers → heat equation

burgers_shock_inviscid.description: Shock formation in ν=0 Burgers at t = t_c
burgers_shock_inviscid.reason: u becomes multivalued: gradient ∂_x u → -∞ at shock → EML-∞

kolmogorov_length.reason: Rational power of ν and ε = exp(rational·ln(·)) = EML-2

taylor_green_turbulent.description: TG vortex at large Re and large t
taylor_green_turbulent.reason: Turbulence = effective EML-∞ (chaotic, no EML-finite attractor)

ns_vorticity_blowup.description: Vorticity at NS blowup time T*
ns_vorticity_blowup.reason: ‖ω‖ → ∞ at T*: EML-∞ transition (same class as phase transitions)

eml_depth_summary.EML-2: Cole-Hopf transform, Kolmogorov scales, energy dissipation, NS vorticity (smooth), power-law spectrum
eml_depth_summary.EML-3: Burgers solution via Cole-Hopf, Taylor-Green initial condition, heat kernel erf
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: Inviscid shock formation, NS blowup vorticity, turbulence (large Re, large t)

connections.to_session_62: Session 62 NS blowup conjecture: Session 76 deepens with Cole-Hopf + Kolmogorov
connections.to_session_57: Shock/blowup = EML Phase Transition (same theorem as Ising T_c)
connections.to_session_64: Feynman-Kac: heat kernel EML-3 bridge; Cole-Hopf uses same heat kernel
connections.to_session_75: QFT confinement = EML-∞ at Λ_QCD; NS blowup = EML-∞ at T* — same mechanism
